answer:
Yes, Salesforce offers free developer access through the Salesforce Developer Edition [1][2]. This environment is self-serve, requires no contract, and does not expire as long as you log in periodically [2][3]. It includes the full Salesforce platform, providing access to APIs for building and testing applications, integrating external systems, and developing AI agents with Agentforce [1][3]. To check your current API limits within an environment, you can query the REST API Limits resource:

```http
GET /services/data/vXX.X/limits
```

This request returns your maximum and remaining API allocations [4]. You can sign up for a free Developer Edition org directly via the Salesforce developer website [1][2].
